name: Mangosteen (Manggis)
description: A tropical evergreen tree famous for its sweet, tangy, juicy white flesh enclosed in a thick, deep purple rind. Known as the queen of fruits.

name: Durian
description: A large tropical fruit famous for its formidable spiky husk and rich, custard-like flesh with a famously strong, pungent aroma.

name: Snake Fruit (Salak)
description: A palm fruit covered in scaly, reddish-brown skin resembling snake skin, containing crunchy, sweet, and slightly acidic segments.

name: Rambutan
description: A medium-sized tropical tree producing bright red or yellow fruits covered in soft, hair-like spines, with sweet, translucent white flesh.

name: Jackfruit (Nangka)
description: The largest tree-borne fruit in the world, featuring a thick spiky rind and sweet, yellow, highly aromatic bulbs used ripe or cooked green.

name: Soursop (Sirsak)
description: A tropical fruit with a dark green, prickly skin and soft, fibrous white pulp that is delightfully sweet and sour, perfect for juices.

name: Duku / Langsat
description: A sweet, grape-like tropical fruit growing in clusters, featuring a thin leathery skin and translucent, sweet-sour segmented flesh.

name: Dragon Fruit (Buah Naga)
description: A climbing cactus fruit with striking pink or yellow skin and sweet, seed-speckled white or deep red flesh, rich in antioxidants.

name: Pineapple (Nanas Madu)
description: A sweet, juicy tropical fruit with a spiky crown and golden flesh, known for its honey-like sweetness and rich flavor when grown in sunny areas.

name: Kintamani Orange (Jeruk)
description: A famous Balinese highland citrus known for its sweet-tart flavor, thin skin, and bright orange color when grown in cool climates.

name: Bali Pomelo (Jeruk Bali)
description: A large citrus fruit with thick rind and sweet, juicy, pinkish-white vesicles, thriving in warm and sunny lowland areas of Bali.

name: Passionfruit (Markisa)
description: A climbing vine producing highly aromatic, sweet-tart fruit filled with juicy seeds, thriving in cool upland valleys.

name: Strawberries (Stroberi)
description: Delicious red berries cultivated in cool, misty mountain regions like Bedugul, requiring lower temperatures to develop sweetness.

name: Water Apple (Jambu Air)
description: A crisp, bell-shaped tropical fruit with high water content and a mild sweet flavor, thriving in hot, wet lowland conditions.

name: Starfruit (Belimbing)
description: A star-shaped tropical fruit that is juicy, crunchy, and text-sweet, highly suited to warm, sun-drenched coastal and lowland plains.

name: Mango (Mangga)
description: A luscious, sweet tropical fruit requiring hot dry seasons to flower and mature, producing best on Bali's sunny northern and southern plains.

name: Papaya (Pepaya)
description: A fast-growing herb producing sweet, orange-fleshed melons year-round, thriving in warm climates with well-drained soils.

name: Banana (Pisang)
description: A versatile tropical staple crop with fast-growing giant herbs that yield rich banana bunches across Bali's lowlands and hills.

name: Bali Berry (Kepundung)
description: Also known as Kepundung or Menteng, this native Bali fruit is small, sour-sweet, and rich in vitamin C, growing in clusters on woody trees.

name: Avocado (Alpukat)
description: A nutrient-dense fruit with a rich, buttery texture. It thrives in cooler highland climates with well-drained volcanic soils.

name: Coconut (Kelapa)
description: The iconic tropical palm of Bali's coastlines, providing refreshing water, rich cream, and oil. Essential for local daily life and ceremonies.

name: Guava (Jambu Biji)
description: A hardy tropical fruit with pink or white flesh, rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber. Highly adaptable but prefers warm lowlands.

name: Breadfruit (Sukun)
description: A versatile tropical fruit with starchy, white flesh, typically eaten fried or boiled. Thrives in humid, low-elevation areas.

name: Coffee - Arabica (Kopi Arabika)
description: Premium highland coffee prized for its complex, aromatic, and acidic profile. Thrives in cool temperatures and volcanic soils.

name: Coffee - Robusta (Kopi Robusta)
description: A bold, strong coffee with high caffeine and low acidity. Thrives in warmer, mid-elevation environments, showing excellent disease resistance.

name: Cacao (Kakao)
description: The source of chocolate, this tropical tree requires constant warmth, shade, and humidity. Thrives in lowland alluvial soils.

name: Cashew (Kacang Mete)
description: A drought-tolerant tree producing premium nuts and cashew apples. Highly suited to the dry, warm coastal soils of northern and eastern Bali.

name: Vanilla (Vanili)
description: Vanilla is a climbing orchid cultivated for its aromatic pods, thriving in humid, filtered shade and requiring careful hand-pollination.

name: Cloves (Cengkeh)
description: Cloves are aromatic dried flower buds of an evergreen tree, widely grown in Bali's wet hillsides for spice and cigarette production.

name: Palm Sugar / Aren
description: An adaptable, versatile palm tapped for its sweet sap to produce traditional palm sugar (gula aren) and alcoholic drinks.

name: Rice (Padi)
description: Bali's agricultural staple, grown in stunning terraced subak systems from coastal plains to cool mountain valleys.

name: Tamarind (Asam Jawa)
description: A large, drought-tolerant, slow-growing tree producing sour, pulp-filled pods used widely in Indonesian cooking and drinks.

name: Bali White Mango (Wani)
description: A highly prized local white mango with smooth, fragrant, and juicy white flesh, eaten fresh or used in spicy sambals.

name: Custard Apple (Srikaya)
description: A small, sweet tropical fruit with knobby green skin and creamy, custard-like white pulp surrounding black seeds.

name: Kaffir Lime (Jeruk Purut)
description: A highly aromatic citrus shrub prized for its bumpy, oil-rich fruit and double-lobed leaves used in Southeast Asian cuisine.

name: Buni Berry (Buni)
description: A native evergreen tree bearing large grape-like clusters of berries that transition from green to red and dark purple.
